summary: Large mechanical assembly cleanup replacing hardcoded small-data base expressions like lbl-_SDA_BASE_(r13) and lbl-_SDA2_BASE_(r2) with the assembler relocation form lbl@sda21(r13/r2), plus bare addi forms using lbl@sda21. The PR touched 210 assembly files across MSL, Dolphin SDK, sysdolphin/baselib, and many Melee game systems with near-equal added/deleted line counts, indicating a broad syntax normalization rather than behavioral code changes.
what_changed: Replaced uses of labels subtracted from _SDA_BASE_ with @sda21 relocations, e.g. lwz r0, lbl_804D7060-_SDA_BASE_(r13) became lwz r0, lbl_804D7060@sda21(r13).
Replaced uses of labels subtracted from _SDA2_BASE_ with @sda21 relocations, e.g. lfd f0, lbl_804DE160-_SDA2_BASE_(r2) became lfd f0, lbl_804DE160@sda21(r2).
Updated store and load instructions consistently, including lwz, stw, lfs, lfd, and similar SDA-relative forms shown in the diff excerpt.
Updated non-parenthesized immediate/address forms such as addi r4, r13, lbl_804D5B48-_SDA_BASE_ to addi r4, r13, lbl_804D5B48@sda21.

Applied the same syntax cleanup broadly across 210 assembly files, with 45543 added and 45549 deleted lines, suggesting the intended effect was source representation cleanup while preserving generated machine code.
smart_moves: Used the standard @sda21 relocation syntax instead of manually spelling offsets from _SDA_BASE_ and _SDA2_BASE_, making small-data references less brittle and less dependent on hardcoded base-symbol arithmetic.
Applied the cleanup uniformly across both r13 SDA and r2 SDA2 references, avoiding a half-converted state where future assembly or decomp work would need to handle two equivalent notations.
Preserved the original instruction bytes in the assembly comments in the shown diff, which provides immediate evidence that the syntax change is intended to assemble to the same instructions.
Kept the change mechanical and broad, which is appropriate for a repository-wide assembler syntax convention fix.
decomp_lessons: Prefer symbolic assembler relocation modifiers such as @sda21 over hand-written label minus _SDA_BASE_ or _SDA2_BASE_ expressions for small-data-area references.
For PowerPC small data in this project, r13-backed SDA and r2-backed SDA2 references can both be expressed with @sda21 using the appropriate base register in the operand.
Mechanical assembly normalization can improve maintainability without changing matching output when instruction encodings are preserved.
When replacing raw assembly with C later, normalized SDA syntax makes it easier to identify genuine small-data/global accesses rather than opaque hardcoded arithmetic against base symbols.
Large repository-wide syntax migrations should be treated as convention changes, not as evidence of behavioral fixes, unless comments or diffs show semantic changes.
naming_conventions: Use label@sda21(r13) for references formerly written as label-_SDA_BASE_(r13).
Use label@sda21(r2) for references formerly written as label-_SDA2_BASE_(r2).
Use label@sda21 as the immediate expression in addi-style forms when the base register is already an operand, e.g. addi r4, r13, label@sda21.
The PR did not introduce new symbol names; existing lbl_-address-style labels were retained.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Convert SDA-relative load/store operands from explicit base subtraction to @sda21 relocation syntax while keeping the same base register.
For r13 accesses, map _SDA_BASE_ expressions to @sda21(r13).
For r2 accesses, map _SDA2_BASE_ expressions to @sda21(r2).
Check that the instruction byte comments remain unchanged after the textual rewrite to confirm the assembler emits the same opcodes.
This kind of cleanup is suitable for scripted or search-and-replace style migration, but should include both parenthesized and bare immediate forms.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available beyond the PR title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining uses of _SDA_BASE_ or _SDA2_BASE_ in assembly after PR 86.
Search for @sda21(r13) and @sda21(r2) examples when converting assembly globals to C declarations.
Check whether any assembler or build-script changes around this date enabled @sda21 relocation syntax.
Compare object output before and after this PR to confirm no instruction encoding changes for normalized SDA references.
